sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after green grow
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
items = [n for n in seg if n['color'] in ['Y', 'N', 'O', 'S'] and n['pixels'] > 10]
print("Colored items:")
for s in sorted(items, key=lambda x: x['pixels'], reverse=True):
    print(f"  color={s['color']} pixels={s['pixels']} boundary={s['boundary']}")

# Red dots
reds = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'r']
print(f"\nRed dots: {len(reds)}")
for r in reds:
    print(f"  boundary={r['boundary']}")

# Check purple objects
p_objects = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'p']
print(f"\nPurple objects: {len(p_objects)}, total pixels={sum(p['pixels'] for p in p_objects)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

But the green grow action seems to grow the N item downward, not rightward. Let me try growing it more to see if it eventually grows rightward.
Actually, looking at the image, the green item is now touching the bottom of the right vertical bar. The red dot is at (43, 40), which is near the bottom of the N item.
